Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Criminal Code (mischief related to memorials to first responders)
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Criminal Code (mischief related to memorials to first responders)

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Criminal Code to provide for the offence of committing mischief in relation to a monument or similar structure that honours first responders.
Whereas first responders regularly put their own lives and physical, emotional and mental well-being at risk in order to save the lives of others;
Whereas, for their selfless heroism, first responders are honoured and commemorated with various memorials, statues, cenotaphs and other structures throughout Canada;
Whereas vandalism against these memorials, statues, cenotaphs and other structures dishonours the memory of these first responders and their service to the community;
And whereas significant penalties should be imposed against any person convicted of mischief against a memorial, statue, cenotaph or other structure honouring first responders;
Now, therefore, Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: Section 430 of the Criminal Code is amended by adding the following after subsection (4.11):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Mischief related to memorials to first responders
Subsection.Text: Everyone who commits mischief in relation to property that is a building or structure or part of a building or structure that primarily serves as a monument to honour first responders â including a statue or monument â or an object associated with honouring or remembering those persons if that object is located in or on the grounds of such a building or structure, or a cemetery is guilty of an indictable offence or an offence punishable on summary conviction and is liable,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: whether the offence is prosecuted by indictment or punishable on summary conviction, to the following minimum punishment:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: for a first offence, to a fine of not less than $1,000,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: for a second offence, to imprisonment for not less than 14 days, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: for each subsequent offence, to imprisonment for not less than 30 days;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the offence is prosecuted by indictment, to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the offence is punishable on summary conviction, to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 18 months.

Subsection.Text: In subsection (4.12), first responders means any persons who provide emergency services, including police officers, firefighters and paramedics, and includes members of the Canadian Forces who provide those services.
